message: Node started: Identify the smallest prime p such that the congruence n^4 + 1 is congruent to 0 modulo p has at least one integer solution n. For each candidate prime p, check if there exists an n such that n^4 is congruent to -1 modulo p. Once the smallest such p is found, determine if any of its roots n modulo p can be lifted to a root modulo p^2 using Hensel's Lemma. A root n modulo p lifts to a root modulo p^2 if the derivative of f(n) = n^4 + 1, which is 4n^3, is not congruent to 0 modulo p. Return the value of the smallest prime p that satisfies the condition that n^4 + 1 is congruent to 0 modulo p^2 for some n.
